title: GNC vs Gasoline Savings 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Annual GNC vs Gasoline Fuel Cost Savings
description: Calculate annual fuel savings: gasoline vs CNG. Compare costs and see how much you could save with compressed natural gas.
intro: This calculator compares the annual fuel cost of running a **CNG (Compressed Natural Gas) vehicle** versus a conventional **gasoline-powered vehicle**, giving you a precise dollar-amount savings figure for any 12-month period. It works by dividing your yearly kilometers driven by each fuel's efficiency rating to find total consumption, then multiplying by current fuel prices. The core insight: CNG typically costs 40–65% less per energy-equivalent unit than gasoline, meaning high-mileage drivers can recover a CNG conversion kit investment in 1–3 years. Use this tool whenever you're evaluating a CNG conversion, comparing two vehicles, or projecting fleet operating budgets.
keyTakeaway: **Annual Savings = (km/year ÷ km/L × $/L) − (km/year ÷ km/m³ × $/m³)**. At 20,000 km/year, gasoline at $20,000/L-equivalent and CNG at $8,000/m³-equivalent, you save **$12,000 per year** — enough to offset a typical CNG conversion in under 24 months.
useCases: A commuter driving 20,000 km/year wants to know if a $25,000 CNG conversion kit pays itself off before the 3-year warranty expires.
A small business owner running a 10-vehicle delivery fleet needs to project annual fuel savings to justify a bulk CNG conversion contract.
A used-car buyer is comparing two identical sedans — one gasoline, one factory CNG — and needs the breakeven mileage to choose wisely.
A rideshare driver clocking 60,000 km/year calculates whether switching to CNG cuts enough from monthly operating costs to increase net earnings by 20%+.
A fleet manager for a municipal bus company models how a phased CNG transition over 3 years affects the annual operating budget.
seoKeywords: how much can I save with CNG

fields.label: Gasoline km/L
fields.help: Fuel efficiency: kilometers per liter of gasoline.

fields.label: GNC km/m³
fields.help: Fuel efficiency: kilometers per cubic meter of compressed natural gas.

explanation:
## How It's Calculated

The calculator uses two parallel cost formulas — one for gasoline, one for CNG — then subtracts to find net savings:

```
# Step 1 — Annual gasoline consumption (liters)
Gasoline_Liters = km_per_year / km_per_liter

# Step 2 — Annual CNG consumption (m³)
CNG_m3 = km_per_year / km_per_m3

# Step 3 — Annual fuel costs
Annual_Gasoline_Cost = Gasoline_Liters × price_per_liter
Annual_CNG_Cost      = CNG_m3        × price_per_m3

# Step 4 — Annual savings
Annual_Savings = Annual_Gasoline_Cost − Annual_CNG_Cost

# Step 5 — Payback period (if conversion cost is known)
Payback_Years = Conversion_Cost / Annual_Savings
```

> **Energy note:** 1 m³ of CNG contains roughly **9.0–9.5 kWh** of energy, compared to ~8.9 kWh per liter of gasoline. Because CNG engines are slightly more efficient at converting that energy to motion, the real-world km/m³ figure is typically 85–95% of the km/L figure, not a 1:1 ratio.

---

## Reference Table

The table below shows typical efficiency and cost benchmarks for common vehicle classes. Prices are illustrative reference ranges; always enter your local current prices for accurate results.

| Vehicle Class | Typical km/L (Gasoline) | Typical km/m³ (CNG) | CNG cost as % of gasoline cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| Compact sedan (1.4–1.6 L) | 12–15 km/L | 11–14 km/m³ | 38–52% |
| Mid-size sedan (1.8–2.0 L) | 9–12 km/L | 8–11 km/m³ | 38–52% |
| SUV / Pickup (2.5–3.5 L) | 7–10 km/L | 6–9 km/m³ | 38–52% |
| Taxi / Rideshare (high cycle) | 10–13 km/L | 9–12 km/m³ | 38–52% |
| Light delivery van | 8–11 km/L | 7–10 km/m³ | 38–52% |
| Municipal bus (CNG-optimized) | 3–5 km/L | 2.8–4.5 km/m³ | 35–50% |

> Sources: U.S. Department of Energy Alternative Fuels Data Center fuel economy benchmarks; NIST energy unit conversion factors (1 m³ CNG ≈ 0.84 gasoline-liter equivalent on an energy basis).

---

## Typical Cases

### Case 1 — Urban Commuter
- **Inputs:** 20,000 km/year · 12 km/L · 10 km/m³ · $20,000/L · $8,000/m³
- Gasoline cost: 20,000 ÷ 12 × $20,000 = **$33,333,333**
- CNG cost: 20,000 ÷ 10 × $8,000 = **$16,000,000**
- **Annual savings: $17,333,333 (~52% reduction)**
- Payback on a $300,000 conversion kit: **~6 months**

### Case 2 — High-Mileage Rideshare Driver
- **Inputs:** 60,000 km/year · 11 km/L · 10 km/m³ · $1.80/L (USD equiv.) · $0.80/m³
- Gasoline cost: 60,000 ÷ 11 × $1.80 = **$9,818**
- CNG cost: 60,000 ÷ 10 × $0.80 = **$4,800**
- **Annual savings: $5,018 (~51% reduction)**
- Payback on a $3,500 conversion: **~8.4 months**

### Case 3 — Delivery Fleet (10 Vans)
- **Inputs per van:** 40,000 km/year · 9 km/L · 8 km/m³ · $1.75/L · $0.75/m³
- Per-van gasoline cost: 40,000 ÷ 9 × $1.75 = **$7,778**
- Per-van CNG cost: 40,000 ÷ 8 × $0.75 = **$3,750**
- **Per-van savings: $4,028/year**
- **Fleet savings (10 vans): $40,280/year**
- Payback on $35,000 total conversion: **< 11 months**

---

## Common Mistakes

1. **Using a 1:1 km/L to km/m³ efficiency ratio.** CNG and gasoline have different volumetric energy densities. 1 m³ of CNG ≈ 0.84 liters of gasoline in energy terms. Always measure or look up your specific vehicle's CNG fuel economy separately — don't just copy the gasoline figure.

2. **Ignoring maintenance cost differences.** CNG burns cleaner (lower carbon deposits), which typically extends oil change intervals by 15–25% and reduces exhaust system wear. Omitting this understates total CNG savings.

3. **Forgetting the CNG tank weight penalty.** High-pressure CNG cylinders add 60–120 kg to a vehicle, reducing payload capacity and slightly increasing fuel consumption. For commercial vehicles, recalculate km/m³ after installation.

4. **Not accounting for CNG station availability.** If the nearest CNG station is 15 km out of route, each detour adds real cost and time. Factor detour distance into your annual km total.

5. **Using pump price instead of energy-equivalent price.** Comparing $/liter of gasoline to $/m³ of CNG is already a valid volume-to-volume comparison for this calculator — but be aware that media headlines often quote CNG in $/GGE (gasoline gallon equivalent) which is a different unit; don't mix them.

6. **Assuming constant prices for multi-year projections.** CNG and gasoline prices are both volatile. For payback calculations beyond 1 year, run a sensitivity analysis with ±20% price scenarios.

---

## Related Calculators

No related calculators are currently linked for this topic. Check [hacecuentas.com](/) for the latest tools on vehicle costs, fuel efficiency, and fleet budgeting.

faq.q: What is the energy equivalence between 1 m³ of CNG and 1 liter of gasoline?
faq.a: According to NIST energy conversion standards, 1 m³ of CNG contains approximately 36.0–37.8 MJ of energy, while 1 liter of gasoline contains about 32–34 MJ. On a pure energy basis, 1 m³ CNG ≈ 1.09–1.11 liters of gasoline equivalent. However, because CNG engines combust more completely, real-world vehicle efficiency narrows the gap to roughly 0.90–1.05 km/m³ per km/L.

faq.q: How long does a typical CNG conversion payback take?
faq.a: For a private passenger vehicle driving 20,000–25,000 km/year, payback typically ranges from 18 to 36 months depending on the price spread between CNG and gasoline and the conversion kit cost ($2,500–$5,000 USD for a bi-fuel system). High-mileage vehicles (60,000+ km/year, e.g., taxis) can achieve payback in as little as 6–10 months. Fleet conversions with volume discounts on kits tend to break even within 12 months.

faq.q: Does CNG affect engine performance or horsepower?
faq.a: Yes, moderately. CNG has a higher octane rating (~120 RON) than regular gasoline (~87–93 RON), which allows better knock resistance, but CNG's lower volumetric energy density typically reduces peak horsepower by 10–15% on naturally aspirated engines running a bi-fuel conversion. Factory-optimized CNG engines (e.g., Honda Civic GX, Fiat Panda Natural Power) are tuned to recover most of this loss and may show only 5–8% power reduction.

faq.q: Is CNG safer than gasoline in a crash?
faq.a: CNG tanks are engineered to withstand pressures of 200–250 bar (3,000–3,600 psi) and must pass rigorous impact, fire, and burst tests per NGV2 (ANSI/CSA America) standards. Because CNG is lighter than air, it disperses upward in an open-air leak rather than pooling on the ground like gasoline vapors. The U.S. Department of Transportation classifies properly certified CNG vehicles as no more dangerous than gasoline vehicles for road use.

faq.q: How do CNG prices compare to gasoline prices historically in the US?
faq.a: The U.S. Department of Energy Alternative Fuels Data Center tracks CNG pricing in $/GGE (gasoline gallon equivalent). Historically from 2015–2024, CNG averaged $2.00–$2.60/GGE while regular gasoline averaged $2.40–$3.80/GGE — a persistent 25–50% discount for CNG. The price gap widens during oil price spikes (e.g., 2022: gasoline ~$4.50/GGE vs. CNG ~$2.50/GGE, a 44% saving).

faq.q: Can I use this calculator for bi-fuel vehicles that switch between CNG and gasoline?
faq.a: Yes — run the calculator twice. First, calculate costs assuming 100% CNG use, then 100% gasoline use. Then blend results by the percentage of km driven on each fuel. For example, if you drive 70% on CNG and 30% on gasoline over 20,000 km/year, calculate CNG cost for 14,000 km and gasoline cost for 6,000 km, then add them. This is the standard method recommended by fleet management accounting frameworks.

faq.q: Does CNG reduce CO₂ emissions compared to gasoline?
faq.a: Yes. The U.S. EPA reports that CNG produces approximately 15–20% fewer CO₂ emissions per kilometer than gasoline (roughly 135–155 g CO₂/km for CNG vs. 160–200 g CO₂/km for gasoline in comparable vehicles). CNG also emits virtually zero particulate matter (PM2.5) and lower NOx than gasoline, benefits that the EPA and CARB (California Air Resources Board) recognize in emissions credit programs for fleet operators.

faq.q: What happens to the CNG savings calculation if gasoline efficiency changes after conversion?
faq.a: A bi-fuel CNG conversion adds 60–120 kg of cylinder weight, which can reduce your baseline gasoline km/L by 3–7% due to the extra load. Always re-test or re-estimate your gasoline efficiency *after* installing the CNG system. Entering the pre-conversion km/L figure into this calculator will slightly overstate gasoline costs and thus overstate your net savings by a small but measurable margin.
